No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>

You are a data engineer maintaining a linear audit history using `jj`. You have a repository at `/home/user/audit-repo`.

The repository contains a main line of commits and a few abandoned, divergent, or WIP commits that are cluttering the history.

Your task is to:
1. Identify all commits that are authored by `test@example.com` and have descriptions starting with `WIP:`.
2. Abandon all of these WIP commits using a single `jj abandon` command combined with a revset.
3. Make sure the `main` bookmark points to the latest non-WIP commit, which has the description `Audit log entry 3`.
4. Create a new commit on top of `main` with the description `Audit log entry 4` and add a file `entry4.txt` with content `data`.
I'll start by exploring the repository to understand its current state.

todos.content: Explore the repository and understand current state
Identify all WIP commits authored by test@example.com
Abandon all WIP commits using a single jj abandon with revset
Ensure main bookmark points to 'Audit log entry 3'
Create new commit on top of main with 'Audit log entry 4' and entry4.txt

parts.text:
The repository state is clear. I can see:
- Two WIP commits (`WIP: data ingest part 1` and `WIP: data ingest part 2`) by `test@example.com`
- The `main` bookmark already points to `Audit log entry 3`

Let me proceed with the tasks.

todos.content: Explore the repository and understand current state
Identify all WIP commits authored by test@example.com
Abandon all WIP commits using a single jj abandon with revset
Ensure main bookmark points to 'Audit log entry 3'
Create new commit on top of main with 'Audit log entry 4' and entry4.txt

parts.text: Now I'll abandon the WIP commits using a revset that matches commits authored by `test@example.com` with descriptions starting with `WIP:`.
The regex syntax might differ. Let me check jj's revset syntax for description matching.

parts.text: The commits are visible with `all()` but the `description()` revset isn't matching. Let me try different approaches.

input.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj log --no-pager -r 'description(exact:"WIP: data ingest part 1")'

parts.text: The `description()` revset isn't matching any commits. These WIP commits might not be part of the default visible set for revsets. Let me check if they're considered "hidden" or if I need to include them explicitly.

output.output:
○  zpsztput test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:10:37 34a97aa9
│  WIP: data ingest part 2
~

○  zpsztput test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:10:37 34a97aa9
│  WIP: data ingest part 2
○  kvkrvwzs test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:10:37 dbde7aa7
│  WIP: data ingest part 1
~

parts.text: I can select them by change ID. Let me check why `description()` doesn't match them — the issue might be that `description()` uses substring matching but these are in `all()` but not in the immutable/mutable set used by description filtering. Let me try with explicit revset.
